title: American Music Tourism Act of 2025
stage_label: Reported by the Committee on Energy and Commerce. H. Rept. 119-69.
plain_summary: This law tells the National Travel and Tourism Office to help promote music places and events to bring more visitors to America. The office must help both Americans and people from other countries learn about and visit music festivals, concert halls, and other important music spots in the United States. The law also says the office should promote sports and recreation activities to international visitors. Every two years, the office has to write a report to Congress about what they have done and what they learned.
last_action_text: Received in the Senate. Read twice. Placed on Senate Legislative Calendar under General Orders. Calendar No. 59.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the American Music Tourism Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Responsibilities of the Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Travel and Tourism
sections.text: Part A changes the rules for promoting travel inside the United States. The Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Travel and Tourism must now find places and events in America that are important for music tourism. This person must help make it easier for Americans to travel to these music places and events. Part B changes the rules for bringing visitors from other countries to the United States. The Assistant Secretary must work with other government agencies to bring more business travelers and tourists from other countries to America. This includes helping to bring large meetings and conferences to the United States. The Secretary must focus on rural areas and places with special cultural history or nature tourism as good spots for international meetings and conferences. The Secretary must also help promote travel to sports and recreation events in America. Most importantly for this law, the Secretary must find music tourism locations and events in the United States and help promote international travel to these places. Part C requires new reports to Congress. Starting one year after this law passes, and then every two years after that, the Assistant Secretary must write a report to two Congressional committees. The report must describe activities, findings, achievements, and problems related to all the tourism goals described in this section. Part D adds a new definition to an existing law. Music tourism means two things. First, it means traveling to a state or local area to visit music related attractions like museums, recording studios, concert venues of any size, and other places connected to music. Second, it means traveling to a state or local area to attend music festivals, concerts, live musical performances, or other special music events.
